query:
when deprecating entries with a replacement... DON"T create a new item and separately deprecate the old one with no supersession... this breaks the chain.  There is a proper way to depreccate with supercession... you need to follow that precisely.  Please address this prior to merge: Finding 1 (low, non-blocking): ADR-002 crt-037 not formally superseded

Unimatrix entry #3955 (ADR-002 crt-037: Combined Cap with Informs Second-Priority Ordering) still describes the removed combined-cap design as the current approach. The fix-agent noted it is superseded but did not deprecate or correct the entry. Future agents receiving a briefing may act on stale design intent.

Recommendation: deprecate or correct entry #3955 to reference the new independent-cap design. The pattern anti-pattern entry #3969 captures the fix lesson, but the original ADR-002 needs to be retired.

entries.title: Use context_correct to supersede an ADR — never context_deprecate then context_store separately
ADR-004 col-031: Activate w_phase_explicit at 0.05; AC-16 Non-Separable from AC-12 (superseded by #3688)
ADR correction cascade: changing an ADR requires revalidating all six dependent design documents
